creator: ohauer@FreeBSD.org
text:
On 2011-07-03 16:46, Max Brazhnikov wrote:
> On Sun, 03 Jul 2011 15:30:08 +0200, Olli Hauer wrote:
>> On 2011-07-03 15:11, Max Brazhnikov wrote:
>>> Hi Olli,
>>>
>>> Abbreviations like pl, py, ja looks unclear to me. Any reason to not use
>>> simple perl, python and java?
>>>
>>> Btw, have you announced your patch on mainlist? Some people may want to
>>> review it.
>>>
>>> Thanks,
>>> Max
>>
>> Hi Max,
>>
>> the only reason for me to not use the default perl python ... was portlint.
> 
> In this case portlint can be ignored or it should be taught to not emit false 
> warning.
> 
>> If we can teach portlint not to complain about this keywords all the time
>> I'm happy to change them.
>>
>> Which mainlist do you mean?
>> I'm happy for every reviewer, patches, comment ...
> 
> ports-committers@freebsd.org or ports@freebsd.org, which you like more.
> 
> Max

Hm, I think ports-comitters@ is maybe the better list.
I suspect subversion-freebsd is not wildly uses outside committers.

Anyway I think on ports@ I can get more feedback ...

Will write later a mail to ports@

Thanks,
olli
